records.ety: [L. frustratus, p. p. of frustrare, frustrari, to deceive, frustrate, fr. frustra in vain, witout effect, in erorr, prob. for frudtra and akin to fraus, E. fraud.]

defs: Vain; ineffectual; useless; unprofitable; null; void; nugatory; of no effect.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Frustrated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Frustrating.]

defs: To bring to nothing; to prevent from attaining a purpose; to disappoint; to defeat; to baffle; as, to frustrate a plan, design, or attempt; to frustrate the will or purpose.
quotes: Shall the adversary thus obtain His end and frustrate thine ?

defs: To make null; to nullify; to render invalid or of no effect; as, to frustrate a conveyance or deed.
